key_insight: In an open-label pilot, a single 25 mg psilocybin dose given with multidisciplinary palliative care support appeared safe, feasible, and highly acceptable for demoralized cancer patients with chronic pain.
clinical_relevance: If confirmed in larger studies, this approach could offer a supportive-care option to improve existential distress and pain in outpatient palliative oncology settings.
method: Single 25 mg oral dose with preparation, monitoring, and integration
Outcomes tracked: safety, feasibility, acceptability, demoralization, and pain
evidence_signal: Among the 10 dosed participants, there were no serious adverse events, complete visit retention, and strong self-reported improvements by study end, including lower demoralization and pain scores.
watch_item: This was a small open-label pilot without a control group, so efficacy conclusions are preliminary and implementation would require specialized staffing and careful monitoring.
paper_title: Psilocybin-Assisted Early Palliative Care for Demoralization and Chronic Pain: An Open-Label Pilot Study
